current_fact.fact_text: In 1796, a young Napoleon Bonaparte won his first major victory as an army commander at the Battle of Montenotte. By splitting the Austrian and Piedmontese forces, he turned a cautious campaign into the beginning of his legend as a master of fast, surprising attacks.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

current_fact.match_reason: Napoleon’s aggressive, revolutionary tactics resonate with the episode’s themes of rupture, strategy, and confronting established power.

queue.fact_text: On April 12, 1204, Crusader armies of the Fourth Crusade breached the towering walls of Constantinople and entered the Byzantine capital. Instead of reaching Jerusalem, they spent days looting what was then one of the world’s richest cities, scattering relics and art across Europe.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A special journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and her guest Almudena from Madrid, Spain. Exploring the roots and evolution of this emotionally raw and culturally rich tradition, from its early vocal origins and Andalusian influences, through its transformation on stage and screen, to its modern reinventions by iconic Spanish artists.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

queue.match_reason: The Crusades reshaped Mediterranean culture, including Spain; an episode on flamenco’s layered history echoes centuries of conflict, migration, and hybrid identity.

queue.fact_text: In 1159, after forcing the submission of Prince Raynald of Châtillon, Byzantine emperor Manuel I Komnenos entered Antioch in a triumphal procession. For a moment, the old empire reasserted its glittering authority over a Crusader city built on uneasy alliances.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

queue.match_reason: Antioch was a key Mediterranean crossroads between Greek, Turkish, and Latin worlds; this cross-coastal musical voyage mirrors that layered shoreline of cultures.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 627, King Edwin of Northumbria was baptized into Christianity by Paulinus, Bishop of York. The ceremony, performed in the north of England, marked a turning point in the island’s slow transformation from pagan kingdoms to a Christian landscape of monasteries and stone churches.

episode.title: Morning Drops #28 - Under Scottish Skies
episode.description: Gaia shares a personal moment of transition — a shift in seasons, in rhythm, and in life. From this space of quiet change comes a musical journey through Scotland: not the usual names, but voices that speak from the edges. A personal ritual and a small act of curiosity: an invitation to notice what grounds us when everything moves.
songs: Paolo Nutini - Pencil Full of Lead

queue.match_reason: A king’s spiritual shift in northern Britain aligns with an episode about quiet transitions and Celtic-tinged landscapes under northern skies.

queue.fact_text: In 467, Anthemius was elevated to emperor of the Western Roman Empire, a title that came with more troubles than power. Surrounded by invading tribes, rival generals, and a shrinking treasury, his short reign shows how the word "emperor" sometimes hides a crumbling world.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: A fading empire clinging to old power mirrors our own uneasy grip on digital empires, making the reflection on dependence and disconnection feel timeless.

queue.fact_text: On April 12, 1945, the U.S. Ninth Army crossed the Elbe River near Magdeburg, reaching Tangermünde just 80 kilometers from Berlin in the final weeks of World War II in Europe. The crossing symbolized both the collapse of Nazi Germany and the approaching Cold War divide.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: A crossing that signaled the end of a devastating war and the start of a new era fits an episode about looking back on difficult transitions and healing forward.

queue.fact_text: In 1999, during the NATO bombing of Yugoslavia, a U.S. F‑15E Strike Eagle attacked a passenger train at the Grdelica gorge, killing dozens of civilians. The tragedy, captured on cockpit video, sparked fierce debate over precision warfare and what "collateral damage" really means.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: A controversial act of modern warfare and civilian loss fits with Residente’s raw, political storytelling about power, violence, and accountability.

queue.fact_text: On April 12, 1990, Widerøe Flight 839, a small Norwegian commuter plane, crashed shortly after takeoff from Værøy Airport, killing all five on board. The intense winds and turbulence around the island made the airfield so dangerous that the airport was permanently closed afterward.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: Both the crash and the episode unfold in Norway’s dramatic, wind-swept north, contrasting human fragility in the air with the quiet grace of life in Arctic waters.

queue.fact_text: In 1877, the United Kingdom formally annexed the Transvaal, absorbing the Boer republic into the British Empire. The move, resisted by many local settlers, set the stage for rising tensions and eventually the South African wars that would shake the region.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: A story of British colonial expansion in southern Africa pairs with a celebration of African musical resistance and self-expression born from postcolonial realities.

queue.fact_text: On April 12, 2009, Zimbabwe officially abandoned its hyperinflated Zimbabwean dollar, legalizing foreign currencies instead. After bills had been printed in denominations up to 100 trillion dollars, everyday transactions suddenly ran on U.S. dollars and South African rand instead of wheelbarrows of local cash.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: A nation forced to rethink its entire money system reflects the episode’s theme of painful unlearning, failure, and slow adaptation to a new reality.

queue.fact_text: In 1990, the same year as Jim Gary’s dinosaurs, Valparaíso’s great fire was still decades away, but on this date Widerøe Flight 839 fell from the Norwegian sky. The loss of five lives pushed authorities to rethink how and where airports are built among extreme winds, showing how geography can quietly rewrite aviation rules.

episode.title: Morning Drops #35 - Thailand
episode.description: Recorded in Koh Samui, Thailand, where Yasmin and her friend Patricia spent more than two intense weeks training Muay Thai and Western boxing. Between sweat, rhythm, and sea breeze, they share reflections on discipline, friendship, and the beauty of movement - with a soundtrack that connects Thai sounds to the flow of daily practice.
songs: Burna Boy - Cheat on Me (feat. Dave)

queue.match_reason: Both the accident and this episode explore how environment and movement interact—one in dangerous winds, the other in disciplined bodily motion shaped by climate and place.

queue.fact_text: On April 12, 1990, in a very different corner of the world, Jim Gary’s mechanical dinosaurs strode into the Smithsonian, transforming a temple of natural history into a playground of welded bones and bright colors. Visitors suddenly met the ancient past not through fossils, but through the imagination of a single sculptor.

episode.title: Morning Drops #25 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

queue.match_reason: Gary’s hybrid, future-fossil dinosaurs mirror trip-hop’s cut-and-weld aesthetic, assembling new creatures from recycled industrial parts and sampled fragments.

queue.fact_text: Also on this date in 1782, the Battle of the Saintes echoed across Caribbean waters dotted with coral reefs and whales. As wooden warships traded broadsides, the sea below carried its own ancient traffic—migrating creatures oblivious to empires clashing above.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: Revisiting the same naval battle from the ocean’s perspective pairs well with an episode that centers marine life and the calm world beneath human conflict.

queue.fact_text: On April 12, 1961, while the world cheered Gagarin, engineers on both sides of the Iron Curtain were already sketching the next generation of spacecraft. The Space Race was less a single event than a feedback loop of imagination—each launch a prompt to think again about what a human body and a machine could do together.

episode.title: Morning Drops #52- Think Again
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the quiet art of changing your mind, guided by Adam Grant's book Think Again. From the beliefs we carry without questioning to the identities we build around what we think we know, this episode follows the slow, sometimes uncomfortable process of unlearning.

Along the way, we meet a Serbian trumpet player who had to let go of one dream to find another, a Tunisian oud master who challenged centuries of tradition from the inside, and a Japanese band who found freedom in playing for no one. The music moves from Balkan brass to Mediterranean stillness to psychedelic drift, tracing the shape of a mind opening up.

Listen slowly, and ask yourself: is there something you believe today that deserves a second look?

Tracklist:
Boban Marković Orkestar – SAT
Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Kikagaku Moyo – Dripping Sun
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying

queue.match_reason: The continual rethinking that drove the Space Race aligns with an episode about revising beliefs and pushing beyond first assumptions, whether in science or in life.

published.fact_text: In 1961, Yuri Gagarin orbited Earth in Vostok 1, becoming the first human to travel into outer space. His 108‑minute flight turned a Soviet test pilot into a global icon overnight and forced humanity to rethink its place in the cosmos.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A literal human 'space shift' fits an episode about reimagining emptiness as creative space and taking a bold first step.

published.fact_text: On April 12, 1981, Space Shuttle Columbia launched on STS‑1, the first orbital flight of NASA’s partially reusable shuttle system. It was the first time a crewed spacecraft was sent to space on its very first test flight, blending experimental risk with cinematic ambition.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

published.match_reason: The shuttle’s debut mirrors the launch of a new format: both open a reusable pathway into unexplored space, one orbital and one musical.

published.fact_text: In 1985, Space Shuttle Discovery’s STS‑51D mission lifted off carrying two commercial communications satellites. A stuck antenna on one satellite forced astronauts into an improvised spacewalk using makeshift tools, turning a routine deployment into an orbital repair drama.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

published.match_reason: The mission’s satellite choreography and improvised spacewalk pair well with an evolving, orbit-like musical journey from ambient lift-off to rhythmic communication.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1934, observers atop Mount Washington in New Hampshire recorded a surface wind gust of 231 mph (372 km/h), then the fastest ever measured. For decades, the mountain was known as “Home of the World’s Worst Weather,” a natural wind tunnel for extreme atmospheric experiments.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: A record-shattering mountain gust feels like nature’s own electric shock, matching the high-voltage guitar energy of this episode.

published.fact_text: In 1970, the Soviet nuclear submarine K‑8 sank in the stormy Bay of Biscay after a fire, carrying four nuclear torpedoes to the seafloor. The crew fought smoke, flooding, and radiation for days, turning the Atlantic into a quiet graveyard for a Cold War ghost.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: A nuclear sub lost to the deep is a dark inversion of the episode’s theme of immersion, connecting through the ocean’s quiet, mysterious depths.

published.fact_text: Three years earlier in 1963, the Soviet nuclear-powered submarine K‑33 collided with the Finnish cargo ship M/S Finnclipper in the narrow Danish straits. The accident, shrouded in secrecy for years, briefly turned a busy shipping lane into an underwater Cold War traffic jam.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: A tense Cold War collision in narrow waters contrasts with the episode’s peaceful sailing trip, both set on crowded European sea lanes.

published.fact_text: In 1990, sculptor Jim Gary opened his "Twentieth Century Dinosaurs" exhibition at the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Natural History, becoming the only sculptor ever given a solo show there. His dinosaurs, built from recycled car parts, turned scrap metal into prehistoric creatures that seemed ready to walk again.

episode.title: Morning Drops #19 - Wabi Sabi
episode.description: Wabi-sabi is a story about a handmade mug, a small mistake, and the idea that imperfection can be something to keep—not correct. In this Morning Drop, Gaia shares a moment that didn’t go to plan, and four tracks that echo the same spirit: rough takes, studio accidents, lo-fi textures, and quiet surprises. A soft, thoughtful selection moving between warm electronics, classic riffs, and raw edges.A reminder that not everything needs to be polished. Sometimes, it’s better that way.

published.match_reason: Gary’s scrap-metal dinosaurs embody wabi-sabi: imperfect, recycled fragments reborn as living art, perfectly echoing the episode’s celebration of rough edges.

published.fact_text: On April 12, 1862, a small group of Union raiders stole a Confederate train in Georgia in what became known as the Great Locomotive Chase. For hours, the stolen locomotive General thundered northward while Confederate crews pursued in a wild, improvised relay of trains and even on foot.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

published.match_reason: A high-speed train theft and chase through the countryside pairs naturally with an "on the move" travel soundtrack full of forward momentum.

published.fact_text: In 2014, the Great Fire of Valparaíso swept through the hills of the Chilean port city, killing 16 people and destroying over 2,000 homes. The blaze crawled up steep, colorful neighborhoods, turning one of the Pacific’s most picturesque skylines into a mosaic of ash and resilience.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: A devastating urban fire and the long process of rebuilding call for the episode’s reflective, weather-themed optimism about storms eventually passing.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1782, Admiral George Rodney’s Royal Navy fleet defeated the French under the Comte de Grasse at the Battle of the Saintes, off Dominica. Clever maneuvering and a rare "breaking the line" tactic shattered the French formation and reshaped the naval balance of the Caribbean.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

published.match_reason: A dramatic naval clash in the Caribbean connects with a track literally about sailors and a seafaring mood within an episode about landscapes and journeys.

published.fact_text: In 1796, a young Napoleon Bonaparte won his first major victory as an army commander at the Battle of Montenotte. By splitting the Austrian and Piedmontese forces, he turned a cautious campaign into the beginning of his legend as a master of fast, surprising attacks.

In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
